title: The Sealed Storefront
tagline: A real storefront with no backend: live availability from Issues, AES-sealed orders, and tamper-evident custody — all in your browser.
holy_shit: Three live capabilities weld into one: the menu reads live sold-out labels from GitHub Issues, your order is AES-256-GCM sealed so only the seller's key can open it, and the sealed envelope is SHA-256 stamped into a custody chain so any tamper is instantly provable — no server, no backend, no cloud.
powered_by: IssuesDB reads live sold-out labels (donor: the-menu-that-knows-the-kitchen); rapp-sealed/1.0 AES-256-GCM seals the order (donor: broadcast-the-secret); SHA-256 content-addresses the sealed envelope into a custody chain (donor: chain-of-custody)
weld: IssuesDB availability gate → AES-256-GCM seals the order payload into an RSL1 envelope → SHA-256 content-addresses that envelope into a custody chain link, so the order's journey is both confidential and tamper-evident end-to-end
agent_scenario: When you need a zero-backend storefront with live inventory gating, sealed orders readable only by the seller, and tamper-evident custody of the sealed order envelope from submission through delivery.
author: kody-w + Copilot
